mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in consumer prices and decreases in retail sales indicate stagflationary demand destruction that squeezes corporate margins and depresses real consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between the interaction term and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, a monthly increase in consumer prices signals persistent inflation that forces additional monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that drives corporate revenue growth and earnings acceleration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in the top quartile of industrial production momentum do not outperform the bottom quartile by at least 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 100 over three months signals collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, reducing aggregate demand and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a 100-plus drop in housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month contraction in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate signals broad labor market deterioration that rapidly erodes consumer spending confidence and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 0.75 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises corporate borrowing costs and depresses equity valuations through discount rate expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year window.
